steps.title: Connect the sail to pursuit
steps.content: The hypothesis is that the sail helped Spinosaurus succeed in underwater pursuits of prey capable of quick evasive movements. The strongest support would show that the sail improved a skill needed to follow or catch prey that turns suddenly.

steps.title: Look for maneuverability
steps.content: A model with a sail completing sharp submerged turns in less time would connect the sail directly to underwater maneuverability. Faster turning is relevant because evasive prey would be harder to catch if the predator could not quickly change direction.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant test results
steps.content: Choice B concerns water displacement, Choice C suggests greater energy use, and Choice D shows slower travel; none supports better pursuit of evasive prey. The remaining result shows faster sharp turns while submerged, exactly the predicted advantage. The answer is A.
